body:
## Repository profile
Large repository or monorepo

## Adoption stage
First useful report

## Repository
github.com/kubernetes/apimachinery

## Mutation target
./pkg/api/resource

## Environment
linux amd64, go1.26.0, GitHub Actions

## CervoMutants version
v0.4.2

## Install path
GitHub Action

## Closest rollout playbook followed
Large repository playbook

## Time to first useful report
About 56 seconds in GitHub Actions (job wall time)

## Workflow shape
- policy=ci-balanced
- budget=5m
- workers=2
- max_mutants=10
- sample=deterministic
- coverage_prefilter=true
- report surfaces=summary.txt, mutation-report.json, history-dashboard.html, governance-review.md, test-recommendations.md
- baseline-first=false (hosted validation wave)
- quarantine=off

## Mutation signal quality
- healthy denominator behavior: generated=10 covered=10 executed=10 effective=10
- killed=7, survived=3, actionable_review_units=3, ledger_entries=3
- all three survivors were `conditionals-boundary` rows in `pkg/api/resource/amount.go`, so the remaining review friction is about equivalent-risk boundary semantics rather than missing coverage or runtime instability
- this is materially stronger evidence than the older hosted `apimachinery` sample that previously collapsed to zero effective work

## Review UX
- `test-recommendations.md` stayed local and repeatedly pointed first to `pkg/api/resource/amount_test.go`, which is a good sign for large-repo usability
- `governance-review.md` made the three high-equivalent-risk boundary survivors visible as `report-only` audit suggestions instead of suppressing them silently
- the review flow is understandable, but the docs still need to teach that a healthy large-repo first run can surface several equivalent-risk boundary survivors without implying the lane is noisy or broken

## Runtime and resource behavior
- completed successfully with no timeouts, compile errors, or missing artifacts
- resolved the target repository on Go 1.26.0 while the action minimum stayed at 1.25.6

## Rollout friction
- the hosted path itself was healthy because per-repository toolchain resolution already existed in the released action
- the remaining friction is review semantics, not platform setup: high-equivalent-risk boundary survivors can recur even when the large scoped target is otherwise healthy and review-bearing

## Primary blocker class
Signal noise or equivalent-risk survivors

## Suggested outcome
Documentation clarification

## Artifact links or snippets
- run: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/actions/runs/27848829038
- repo job: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/actions/runs/27848829038/job/82423664319
- committed wave note: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/blob/main/docs/evaluations/2026-06-19-external-github-action-wave-v0.4.2-profile-sample.md
- artifact bundle: `external-wave-apimachinery-resource`
- key snippet: `generated=10 effective=10 killed=7 survived=3 actionable_review_units=3 equivalent_risk_survivors=3 go_version=go1.26.0`

## Upstream issue or discussion
None

## External response status
No upstream thread opened

## External response last checked
2026-06-19
title: [Adoption Feedback] kubernetes/apimachinery scoped released GitHub Action report

body:
## Repository profile
Medium service

## Adoption stage
First useful report

## Repository
github.com/prometheus/prometheus

## Mutation target
./model/labels

## Environment
linux amd64, go1.25.6, GitHub Actions

## CervoMutants version
v0.4.2

## Install path
GitHub Action

## Closest rollout playbook followed
Medium service playbook

## Time to first useful report
About 58 seconds in GitHub Actions (job wall time)

## Workflow shape
- policy=ci-balanced
- budget=5m
- workers=2
- max_mutants=10
- sample=deterministic
- coverage_prefilter=true
- report surfaces=summary.txt, mutation-report.json, history-dashboard.html, governance-review.md, test-recommendations.md
- baseline-first=false (hosted validation wave)
- quarantine=off

## Mutation signal quality
- healthy denominator behavior: generated=10 covered=7 executed=7 effective=7
- killed=7, survived=0, not_covered=3, actionable_review_units=0
- this was not a denominator-collapse case; it was a healthy bounded run with no survivors in the selected slice
- the run therefore produced useful rollout evidence even though it produced no test-recommendation output

## Review UX
- `test-recommendations.md` correctly stayed empty because there were no actionable survivors
- `governance-review.md` still preserved one equivalent-risk audit row, which helps keep the run explainable
- the remaining UX friction is interpretation: a healthy zero-survivor result can look like a non-result unless the reviewer already understands the distinction between low signal and healthy no-action lane output

## Runtime and resource behavior
- completed successfully with no timeouts, compile errors, or missing artifacts
- mutation coverage was 70%, with the remaining gap visible as `not_covered=3`

## Rollout friction
- no install or CI setup failure on the released GitHub Action path
- the main friction is documentation/interpretation, not execution: users need explicit guidance that zero actionable review units can still be a healthy first useful report when denominator health remains good

## Primary blocker class
Review UX or report usability

## Suggested outcome
Documentation clarification

## Artifact links or snippets
- run: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/actions/runs/27848829038
- repo job: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/actions/runs/27848829038/job/82423664310
- committed wave note: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/blob/main/docs/evaluations/2026-06-19-external-github-action-wave-v0.4.2-profile-sample.md
- artifact bundle: `external-wave-prometheus-labels`
- key snippet: `generated=10 effective=7 killed=7 survived=0 not_covered=3 actionable_review_units=0 denominator_health=healthy`

## Upstream issue or discussion
None

## External response status
No upstream thread opened

## External response last checked
2026-06-19
title: [Adoption Feedback] prometheus/prometheus labels scoped released GitHub Action report

body:
## Repository profile
Compact library

## Adoption stage
First useful report

## Repository
github.com/tidwall/gjson

## Mutation target
./...

## Environment
linux amd64, go1.25.6, GitHub Actions

## CervoMutants version
v0.4.2

## Install path
GitHub Action

## Closest rollout playbook followed
Small library playbook

## Time to first useful report
About 77 seconds in GitHub Actions (job wall time)

## Workflow shape
- policy=ci-balanced
- budget=5m
- workers=2
- max_mutants=10
- sample=deterministic
- coverage_prefilter=false
- report surfaces=summary.txt, mutation-report.json, history-dashboard.html, governance-review.md, test-recommendations.md
- baseline-first=false (hosted validation wave)
- quarantine=off

## Mutation signal quality
- healthy denominator behavior: generated=10 covered=10 executed=10 effective=10
- survivors=3, but semantic grouping collapsed them to 2 actionable review units
- actionable_score=77.78%, recommendation_entries=3, ledger_entries=2
- the dominant survivor shape was a repeated `conditionals-boundary` / len-boundary cluster, which is exactly the kind of equivalent-risk pattern the semantic triage layer is supposed to compress

## Review UX
- `test-recommendations.md` pointed first to `gjson_test.go` and clearly told the reviewer to tighten opposite-branch or boundary assertions
- `governance-review.md` surfaced four `report-only` equivalent-risk suggestions, including one killed row, which makes the audit trail explicit but can surprise a first-time reviewer
- this is strong evidence that semantic grouping is doing real work in the released GitHub Action path, not just in fixtures

## Runtime and resource behavior
- completed successfully with no timeouts, compile errors, or missing artifacts
- mutation coverage stayed at 100% under the bounded lane

## Rollout friction
- no install or CI setup failure on the released GitHub Action path
- the remaining friction is interpretation: a healthy run can still show several equivalent-risk boundary rows, so reviewers need the docs to frame `semantic_group_review_units` and `report-only` governance suggestions correctly

## Primary blocker class
Signal noise or equivalent-risk survivors

## Suggested outcome
Documentation clarification

## Artifact links or snippets
- run: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/actions/runs/27848829038
- repo job: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/actions/runs/27848829038/job/82423664324
- committed wave note: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/blob/main/docs/evaluations/2026-06-19-external-github-action-wave-v0.4.2-profile-sample.md
- artifact bundle: `external-wave-gjson-root`
- key snippet: `generated=10 effective=10 killed=7 survived=3 actionable_review_units=2 semantic_groups=1 collapsed_semantic_duplicates=1`

## Upstream issue or discussion
None

## External response status
No upstream thread opened

## External response last checked
2026-06-19
title: [Adoption Feedback] tidwall/gjson released GitHub Action first useful report

body:
## Repository profile
Compact library

## Adoption stage
First useful report

## Repository
github.com/spf13/pflag

## Mutation target
./...

## Environment
linux amd64, go1.25.6, GitHub Actions

## CervoMutants version
v0.4.2

## Install path
GitHub Action

## Closest rollout playbook followed
Small library playbook

## Time to first useful report
About 40 seconds in GitHub Actions (job wall time)

## Workflow shape
- policy=ci-balanced
- budget=5m
- workers=2
- max_mutants=10
- sample=deterministic
- coverage_prefilter=false
- report surfaces=summary.txt, mutation-report.json, history-dashboard.html, governance-review.md, test-recommendations.md
- baseline-first=false (hosted validation wave)
- quarantine=off

## Mutation signal quality
- healthy denominator behavior: generated=10 covered=10 executed=10 effective=10
- survivors=3, actionable_review_units=3, recommendation_entries=3
- equivalent-risk survivors=1, semantic_group_review_units=1
- the run produced real review work rather than denominator-only evidence

## Review UX
- `test-recommendations.md` stayed local enough to be usable and pointed first to `bool_slice_test.go`, `errors_test.go`, and `flag_test.go`
- `governance-review.md` made the high-equivalent-risk boundary survivor visible as `report-only` instead of treating it as silent noise
- the remaining UX rough edge is that one healthy run can still surface a wide nearby-test list, so the human still needs the first anchor test callout

## Runtime and resource behavior
- completed successfully with no timeouts, compile errors, or missing artifacts
- mutation coverage stayed at 100% under the bounded lane

## Rollout friction
- no install or CI setup failure on the released GitHub Action path
- the main remaining friction was review semantics, not execution: healthy runs can still include equivalent-risk boundary survivors that should be reviewed once rather than treated as separate mandatory tasks

## Primary blocker class
Signal noise or equivalent-risk survivors

## Suggested outcome
Documentation clarification

## Artifact links or snippets
- run: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/actions/runs/27848829038
- repo job: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/actions/runs/27848829038/job/82423664322
- committed wave note: https://github.com/cervantesh/cervo-mutants/blob/main/docs/evaluations/2026-06-19-external-github-action-wave-v0.4.2-profile-sample.md
- artifact bundle: `external-wave-pflag-root`
- key snippet: `generated=10 effective=10 killed=7 survived=3 actionable_review_units=3 equivalent_risk_survivors=1`

## Upstream issue or discussion
None

## External response status
No upstream thread opened

## External response last checked
2026-06-19
title: [Adoption Feedback] spf13/pflag released GitHub Action first useful report
